    "text": "An equation is a statement  in which one expression equals to another expression. An\nequation of the form ax + by + c = 0, where a, b and c are real numbers such that\na ≠ 0 and b ≠ 0, is called a linear equation in two variables. The process of finding\nsolution(s) is called solving an equation.\nThe solution of a linear equation is not affected when\n(i)\nthe same number is added to (subtracted from) both sides of the equation,\n(ii)\nboth sides of the equation are multiplied or divided by the same non-zero\nnumber.\nFurther, a linear equation in two variables has infinitely many solutions. The graph of\nevery linear equation in two variables is a straight line and every point on the graph\n(straight line) represents a solution of the linear equation. Thus, every solution of the\nlinear equation can be represented by a unique point on the graph of the equation. The\ngraphs of x = a and y = a are lines parallel to the y-axis and x-axis, respectively.\n(B) Multiple Choice Questions",

Write the linear equation to\nexpress the above statement. Draw the graph\nof the linear equation.\nSolution : Let the total distance covered be\nx km and the fare charged Rs y. Then for the\nfirst km, fare charged is Rs 10 and for\nremaining (x –1) km fare charged is\nRs 4 (x – 1).\nTherefore, y = 10 + 4(x – 1) = 4x + 6\nThe required equation is y = 4x + 6.     "text": "graph by taking the constant force as 3 units. What is the work done when the distance\ntravelled is 2 units. Verify it by plotting the graph.\nSolution: Work done = (constant force) × (distance)\n= 3 × (distance),\ni.e., y = 3x, where y (units) is the work done and\nx (units) is the distance travelled. Since x = 2 units\n(given), therefore, work done = 6 units. To plot\nthe graph of the linear equation y = 3x, we need at\nleast two solutions of the equation. We see that\nx = 0, y = 0 satisfies the given equation also x = 1,\ny = 3 satisfies the equation.\nNow we plot the points A (0, 0) and B (1, 3) and\njoin AB (see Fig. 4.7). The graph of the equation\nis a straight line. [We have not shown the whole\nline because work done cannot be negative].\nTo verify from the graph, draw a perpendicular to\nthe x-axis at the point (2, 0) meeting the graph at\nthe point C.
